title: C# Basic — Paper 8
description: C# concepts: loops, loop control, arrays, variables, strings, properties, static members, inheritance basics, enums, and structs.

questions.text: What is a `while` loop that never terminates called, and what is the correct pattern in C#?

options.text: An infinite loop; pattern: while (true) { ... break; }
A static loop; no valid pattern exists

questions.text: What does `foreach (var item in collection)` do if the collection is empty in C#?

options.text: Executes the body once with item set to null
Skips the loop body entirely; zero iterations

questions.text: What happens when `break` is used inside a nested loop in C#?

options.text: Only the innermost loop containing the break terminates

questions.text: What is the result of calling `return` in the middle of a `for` loop inside a method?

options.text: The loop stops but the method continues
The method exits immediately returning the specified value

questions.text: What does `Array.Length` property return for `int[] arr = {1, 2, 3, 4, 5}`?
What is the index of the first and last element of an array `arr` with 10 elements in C#?
How do you declare a jagged array of 3 inner arrays in C#?
What is the size of the `long` data type in C#?
What is the type inferred by `var greeting = "Hello";` in C#?
Can you change the value of a `readonly` field after it is set in the constructor in C#?

options.text: Yes, but only from within the same class
No, once set in the constructor it cannot be changed

questions.text: What does `string.Replace("hello", "world")` do in C#?

options.text: Returns a new string with all occurrences of "hello" replaced by "world"

questions.text: What is the result of `"  Hello  ".TrimStart()`?
How do you build a string from parts efficiently in a loop in C#?

options.text: Concatenate with + operator inside the loop

questions.text: What is the difference between `{ get; }` and `{ get; private set; }` on a property in C#?

options.text: `{ get; }` can only be set in a constructor or initializer; `{ get; private set; }` can be set anywhere in the class
`{ get; private set; }` prevents any assignment
`{ get; }` allows writing from derived classes

questions.text: What is the `required` modifier for a property in C# 11+?

options.text: It requires the property to be set during object initialization
It requires the property to have a backing field
It marks the property as non-nullable

questions.text: What does `{ get; init; }` enforce that `{ get; set; }` does not in C# 9+?

options.text: `init` allows the property to be set by derived classes only
`init` restricts setting to construction time; `set` allows setting at any time

questions.text: What is a Singleton pattern and how do static members help implement it in C#?

options.text: A pattern that creates multiple instances; static constructors call each one
A pattern ensuring only one instance of a class exists; a static field holds the single instance
A pattern where all methods are static and no instance is needed
A pattern that reuses objects from a pool using static arrays

questions.text: What does the `static` keyword do when applied to a field?

options.text: Makes the field belong to the class rather than any instance; shared across all objects
Makes the field accessible only within the method it is declared in
Prevents the field from being null

questions.text: What happens if a static constructor throws an exception in C#?

options.text: The exception propagates and the class becomes unusable for the lifetime of the AppDomain
The static constructor retries on next access
A fallback default constructor is called instead

questions.text: What is the `base` keyword used for in an overridden method in C#?

options.text: To call another method in the same class
To call the base class's version of the overridden method
To create an instance of the base class
To access private fields of the base class

questions.text: What does `new` (as a method modifier, not operator) do in C# when used in a derived class?

options.text: Creates a new instance inside the method
Hides the base class method rather than overriding it
Forces the method to call the base class version
Makes the method available only as static

questions.text: Can a C# interface contain default method implementations (C# 8+)?

options.text: No, interfaces can only declare signatures
Yes, C# 8 introduced default interface method implementations
Only abstract methods are allowed in interfaces
Only if the interface is sealed

questions.text: What is the numeric value of `Direction.South` if Direction enum is `{ North, South, East, West }`?
How do you combine two [Flags] enum values in C#?
What does `Enum.GetNames(typeof(Season))` return in C#?

options.text: The integer values of the enum
An array of strings representing the enum member names
A list of all enum types in the assembly

questions.text: Can a struct in C# implement an interface?

options.text: No, only classes can implement interfaces
Only if the interface has no methods
Only if the struct is declared as public

questions.text: Where are struct instances typically stored in C# memory?

options.text: On the stack when declared as local variables (or inline in other value types)

questions.text: What is the primary reason structs are passed by value, not by reference, in C#?

options.text: Structs are value types; assignment and method passing creates a copy of the data
The C# compiler enforces this as a security measure
